claim: CX3CR1 on the Th2 cells impairs T cell survival

result.claim: CX3CR1 on the Th2 cells impairs T cell survival
result.reasoning: The claim that CX3CR1 on Th2 cells impairs T cell survival is refuted by evidence from paper ID 21366394. This paper, titled "CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung," explicitly states that "CX3CR1 signaling promoted T(H)2 survival in the inflamed lungs" (prop_2969). The abstract further reinforces this by stating, "We found that CX3CR1 signaling promoted T(H)2 survival in the inflamed lungs." This directly contradicts the claim, indicating that CX3CR1 promotes, rather than impairs, T cell survival in Th2 cells.

evidence.text: Injection of B cell leukemia/lymphoma-2 protein (BCl-2)-transduced CX3CR1-deficient T(H)2 cells into CX3CR1-deficient mice restored asthma.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: CX3CR1-induced survival was also observed for T(H)1 cells upon airway inflammation.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: The conformational change at CD3 is required for T cell activation.
evidence.source: Full activation of the T cell receptor requires both clustering and conformational changes at CD3.

evidence.text: Transfer of WT CD4(+) T cells into CX3CR1-deficient mice restored the cardinal features of asthma.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: Untreated CX3CR1-deficient mice show reduced lung disease upon allergen sensitization and challenge.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: Wild-type (WT) mice treated with CX3CR1-blocking reagents show reduced lung disease upon allergen sensitization and challenge.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: A fraction of CD4(+) T cells express the CX3CL1 receptor, CX3CR1, in people with asthma.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: CX3CR1 signaling promoted T(H)2 survival in the inflamed lungs.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: CX3CR1-blocking reagents prevented airway inflammation in CX3CR1-deficient recipients injected with WT T(H)2 cells.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung

evidence.text: CX3CR1-induced survival was also observed for T(H)1 cells upon airway inflammation.
evidence.source: CX3CR1 is required for airway inflammation by promoting T helper cell survival and maintenance in inflamed lung
